Treatment Arm(s).value: standard of care plus radiotherapy; standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily); standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' The experimental arms are those that add to the standard of care.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: The 'Findings' section on page 1 states: 'Between Nov 27, 2013, and Dec 20, 2018, 1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data) and assigned to receive standard of care (n=296), standard of care plus radiotherapy (n=293), standard of care plus abiraterone (n=292), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone (n=291).' The experimental arms are 'standard of care plus abiraterone (n=292)' and 'standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone (n=291)'. Summing these gives 292 + 291 = 583. This is further confirmed by the statement: 'In the overall population, patients assigned to receive abiraterone (n=583) had longer radiographic progression-free survival...' on page 1 and in Table 1 under 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=583)' on page 6.

Control Arm - N.evidence: Found in the 'Findings' section on page 1, stating 'assigned to receive standard of care (n=296)'. This is also confirmed in Figure 1, '296 assigned to SOC'.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Findings' section, it states: 'Between Nov 27, 2013, and Dec 20, 2018, 1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data)'. On page 5, in the 'Results' section, it reiterates '1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data)'. Figure 1 also shows '1173 patients enrolled and randomly assigned' with a note '1 withdrew consent for analysis of their data, data not included'. Table 1 on page 6, titled 'Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population', specifies 'Overall population (n=1172)'. This indicates that 1172 participants were included in the analysis after one withdrawal.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states, 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' This describes four distinct treatment arms. The 'Findings' section on page 1 and Figure 1 on page 5 further confirm the assignment to these four arms.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document mentions 'quality of life' as a secondary endpoint in the 'Outcomes' section on page 3 and states on page 9 that 'The impact of toxicity on participants will be better understood after the formal analysis of quality-of-life data'. However, it does not specify a particular 'Quality of Life Scale' used or provide any extracted values for it in the current text.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: On page 3, under the 'Outcomes' section, 'quality of life' is explicitly listed as one of the secondary endpoints: 'The secondary endpoints were castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC)-free survival, serious-genito-urinary-event-free survival, prostate-cancer-specificsurvival, time to next skeletal-related event, PSA response rate, prognostic study of serum PSA measured 6-8 months after initiation of systemic therapy, time to pain pro-gression, time to chemotherapy for CRPC, quality of life, changes in bone mineral density, correlation of biomarkers with outcome, event rate per 100 person-years of treatment analysis, and toxicity.'

Control Arm.value: SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)
Control Arm.evidence: The 'Findings' section on page 1 states that patients assigned to receive abiraterone (n=583) were compared to 'patients who did not receive abiraterone (n=589)'. Table 1 on page 6 explicitly labels this comparison group as 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=589)'. This arm serves as the comparison group that does not receive the experimental treatment (abiraterone).

NCT.evidence: Found in the "Methods" section on page 1, explicitly stating the trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov and providing the ID.

PubMed ID.evidence: The document provides DOIs for the publication but does not explicitly state a PubMed ID for this specific article.

Trial Name.evidence: The trial name "PEACE-1" is explicitly stated in the title of the article on page 1.

Author.evidence: The first author, Karim Fizazi, and a long list of co-authors are provided on page 1, below the title.

Year.evidence: Found on page 1 under "Published Online", indicating the publication date as April 8, 2022.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains a comprehensive structure typical of a full publication, including detailed methods, findings, and discussion, extending over multiple pages.

Phase.evidence: Explicitly stated in the title and the "Methods" section on page 1 as a "phase 3 study".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The "Added value of this study" section on page 2 explicitly states that PEACE-1 is the "first trial to show" these specific findings, indicating it is an original report.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title: 'Abiraterone plus prednisone added to androgen deprivation therapy and docetaxel in de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (PEACE-1)'. It is also mentioned in the 'Methods' section: 'We conducted an open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design (PEACE-1)'.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1: Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population, under 'Overall population (n=1172)', 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=583)' column, row 'Age, years', 'Median'.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1: Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population, under 'Overall population (n=1172)', 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=589)' column, row 'Age, years', 'Median'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document describes the duration of abiraterone treatment as being administered until disease progression, withdrawal of consent, unacceptable toxicity, or death (page 3, 'Procedures' section). However, a specific median duration in months for the treatment arm is not reported in the text or tables.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document describes the duration of abiraterone treatment as being administered until disease progression, withdrawal of consent, unacceptable toxicity, or death (page 3, 'Procedures' section). However, a specific median duration in months for the control arm is not reported in the text or tables.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Abiraterone 1000 mg (four 250 mg tablets, orally) once daily plus prednisone 5 mg orally twice daily, starting within 6 weeks after ADT initiation.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: Found in the 'Procedures' section on page 3, under the paragraph describing the administration of abiraterone. This regimen is a key experimental arm in the 2x2 factorial design study, as highlighted by the study's title and objectives.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 5, in the 'Results' section, it is stated that 'For the overall survival analyses, the median follow-up periods from randomisation to the cutoff date of June 1, 2021, were 4.41 years for the overall population'. This value is converted to months (4.41 years * 12 months/year = 52.92 months).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The 'Introduction' section on page 2 states: 'the treatment of de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer has drastically evolved in the past 10 years'. The 'Methods' section on page 2 further specifies that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic' prostate adenocarcinoma. This indicates that the study focused on and reported results for patients with synchronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The 'Discussion' section on page 11 states: 'Nonetheless, as PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC. Our findings cannot directly address whether this triplet systemic combination is superior to ADT and abiraterone.' This explicitly indicates that the study did not report results based on prognostic groups with metachronous disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The 'Randomisation and masking' section on page 3 states that patient stratification was done 'according to high-volume versus low-volume metastatic burden classification'. Table 1 on page 6 includes 'Metastatic burden' with 'High burden' as a category, showing patient numbers. The 'Results' section on page 6 and 7, and Table 2 on page 9, and Figure 2 (panel F) and Figure 3 (panel B and D) on page 8 and 10, all present outcomes specifically for patients with 'high-volume metastatic burden'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The 'Randomisation and masking' section on page 3 states that patient stratification was done 'according to high-volume versus low-volume metastatic burden classification'. Table 1 on page 6 includes 'Metastatic burden' with 'Low burden' as a category, showing patient numbers. The 'Results' section on page 6 and 7, and Table 2 on page 9, and Figure 2 (panel E) and Figure 3 (panel B and D) on page 8 and 10, all present outcomes specifically for patients with 'low-volume metastatic burden'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population', under the 'ADT with docetaxel population' section, for 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)' and row 'High burden'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population', under the 'ADT with docetaxel population' section, for 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)' and row 'High burden'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population', under the 'ADT with docetaxel population' section, for 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)' and row 'Low burden'.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The study is a 2x2 factorial design investigating the addition of abiraterone and/or radiotherapy to standard of care. The title 'Abiraterone plus prednisone added to androgen deprivation therapy and docetaxel' highlights abiraterone as a primary agent being investigated. The 'Added value of this study' section on page 2 and the 'Discussion' section on page 8 both refer to abiraterone as a 'second-generation androgen signalling inhibitor'. The 'Introduction' section on page 2 also mentions 'second-generation androgen receptor axis inhibitors (abiraterone, apalutamide, or enzalutamide)'. Given the factorial design, 'Treatment Arm 1' is interpreted as referring to one of the primary experimental drug agents being added, which is abiraterone.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: The value '228' is found in Figure 1: Trial profile on page 5, under the '583 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) plus abiraterone groups' section, listed as 'deceased at data cutoff'. This represents the treatment arm. This is further confirmed on page 6, which states 'the addition of abiraterone to SOC (with or without docetaxel and with or without radiotherapy) decreased the number of deaths from 268 to 228', where 228 corresponds to the abiraterone (treatment) group. Table 2 on page 9 also shows '228/583' for 'Overall survival' in the 'SOC with abiraterone groups' for the overall population.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: The value '268' is found in Figure 1: Trial profile on page 5, under the '589 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) without abiraterone groups' section, listed as 'deceased at data cutoff'. This represents the control arm. This is further confirmed on page 6, which states 'the addition of abiraterone to SOC (with or without docetaxel and with or without radiotherapy) decreased the number of deaths from 268 to 228', where 268 corresponds to the without abiraterone (control) group. Table 2 on page 9 also shows '268/589' for 'Overall survival' in the 'SOC without abiraterone groups' for the overall population.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma', which is clarified as 'de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer' in the 'Introduction' on page 2. This indicates that all patients in the study had synchronous metastases. The 'Findings' section on page 1 and Table 1 on page 6 show that 583 patients were in the 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)', which represents the treatment arm for abiraterone efficacy analysis.

The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma', which is clarified as 'de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer' in the 'Introduction' on page 2. This indicates that all patients in the study had synchronous metastases. The 'Findings' section on page 1 and Table 1 on page 6 show that 589 patients were in the 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)', which represents the control arm for abiraterone efficacy analysis.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma', which is clarified as 'de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer' in the 'Introduction' on page 2. This indicates that all metastases were synchronous (at diagnosis), meaning no patients had metachronous metastases at baseline. The treatment arm (SOC plus abiraterone groups) had 583 patients.

The 'Methods' section on page 1 states that eligible patients had 'de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma', which is clarified as 'de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer' in the 'Introduction' on page 2. This indicates that all metastases were synchronous (at diagnosis), meaning no patients had metachronous metastases at baseline. The control arm (SOC without abiraterone groups) had 589 patients.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Derived from Figure 1 on page 5. The total participants in the abiraterone groups (treatment arm) were 292 (SOC plus abiraterone) + 291 (SOC plus abiraterone plus radiotherapy) = 583. Of these, 177 (from SOC plus abiraterone) + 178 (from SOC plus abiraterone plus radiotherapy) = 355 received docetaxel. The percentage is (355/583)*100 = 60.89%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: Derived from Figure 1 on page 5. The total participants in the non-abiraterone groups (control arm) were 296 (SOC) + 293 (SOC plus radiotherapy) = 589. Of these, 178 (from SOC) + 177 (from SOC plus radiotherapy) = 355 received docetaxel. The percentage is (355/589)*100 = 60.27%.

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: The document reports 'CRPC-free survival' which is defined by a confirmed PSA rise (serum androgen progression) among other factors, but it does not explicitly report 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' as a distinct endpoint in months for the treatment arm.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: The document reports 'CRPC-free survival' which is defined by a confirmed PSA rise (serum androgen progression) among other factors, but it does not explicitly report 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' as a distinct endpoint in months for the control arm.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document describes a single randomized controlled trial (PEACE-1) and does not provide an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) for other randomized controlled trials (RCTs). The document itself is an RCT, not a review or meta-analysis assessing other RCTs.

Converted to months (5.1 * 12 = 61.2).

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary outcomes in the ADT with docetaxel population', row 'Overall survival in patients with high-volume metastatic burden', column 'SOC without abiraterone groups', 'Median, years' is 3.5. Converted to months (3.5 * 12 = 42).

Median OS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary outcomes in the ADT with docetaxel population', row 'Overall survival in patients with low-volume metastatic burden', column 'SOC without abiraterone groups', 'Median, years' is NR (Not Reached).

Treatment Class.value: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.
Treatment Class.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.'

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: A specific PFS rate as a percentage for the treatment arm in the overall population is not reported. The document states that patients assigned to receive abiraterone had a median radiographic progression-free survival of 4.5 years (Table 2) and an adjusted HR of 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41-0-71) for abiraterone vs no abiraterone (Page 1, Findings; Page 6, first paragraph).

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: A specific PFS rate as a percentage for the control arm in the overall population is not reported. The document states that patients who did not receive abiraterone had a median radiographic progression-free survival of 2.2 years (Table 2) and an adjusted HR of 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41-0-71) for abiraterone vs no abiraterone (Page 1, Findings; Page 6, first paragraph).

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Primary outcomes in the overall population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival', column 'SOC with abiraterone groups', Median, years = 4.5. Converted to months (4.5 * 12 = 54).

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Primary outcomes in the overall population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival', column 'SOC without abiraterone groups', Median, years = 2.2. Converted to months (2.2 * 12 = 26.4).

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Treatmen.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary outcomes in the ADT with docetaxel population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with high-volume metastatic burden', column 'SOC with abiraterone groups', Median, years = 4.1. Converted to months (4.1 * 12 = 49.2).

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary outcomes in the ADT with docetaxel population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with high-volume metastatic burden', column 'SOC without abiraterone groups', Median, years = 1.6. Converted to months (1.6 * 12 = 19.2).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary outcomes in the ADT with docetaxel population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with low-volume metastatic burden', column 'SOC with abiraterone groups', Median, years = NR (Not Reached).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Secondary outcomes in the ADT with docetaxel population', row 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with low-volume metastatic burden', column 'SOC without abiraterone groups', Median, years = 2.7. Add-on Treatment.value: Docetaxel, abiraterone plus prednisone, and radiotherapy
Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title states 'Abiraterone plus prednisone added to androgen deprivation therapy and docetaxel'. The 'Methods' section on page 1 describes the randomisation arms as 'standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks)', 'standard of care plus radiotherapy', 'standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily)', or 'standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone'. The 'Added value of this study' section on page 2 refers to 'a triple systemic therapy-consisting of ADT, docetaxel, and a second-generation androgen signalling inhibitor (abiraterone, combined with prednisone)'.
